title: Unable to upload file to container over varlink
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

I would like to be able to programmatically upload arbitrary data to a container and put it in a file of my choice. This is possible today in [the Docker API](https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.40/#operation/PutContainerArchive).

In my quest to find a solution to this I scoured the issue tracker and the source and I found two possible solutions:

1. Like `podman cp`, mount, write, unmount.

   This was initially successfull, but only because I was running `podman` on the same machine, as this relies on access to the same filesystem as the podman host. Even when co-located, it would sometimes suffer from permission issues when writing files into the containers filesystem.
2. I found the following snippet in the libpod source:

   https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/pkg/adapter/runtime_remote.go#L550
   While I was able to upload the file (I took the opportunity of adding some helpers to varlink/go: https://github.com/varlink/go/pull/19), but it just gives me the name of a tempfile on the host filesystem, and infuriatingly doesn't seem to allow me to move the file after the transfer has finished.

   This is still my main line of inquiry, and I believe it would be successful if there was just some way to move the file once it has been transmitted. Any pointers?

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Attempt to upload a file or archive to a container over varlink.

**Describe the results you received:**

This was impossible, as best I can tell.

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expected it to be possible, as an analogue to the [Docker archive upload API](https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.40/#operation/PutContainerArchive)

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:            1.6.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.3
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

On bare metal (laptop).

comments.body: No, but I guess that covers the same thing. This is about a library I'm writing for dynamically starting and removing containers for Go tests (https://github.com/uw-labs/podrick).
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
Ok I removed it from auto close.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I am going to close this issue. To the best of my knowledge, there are no plans to support copy over varlink. This will likely change with the upcoming v2 API (rest based).
